subject: My Tax Cut
body:
Over the coming weeks, new, lower tax rates will start taking effect. That means... | | |
---
| |
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ad17edc229&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| **_My Tax Cut_** Over the coming weeks, new, lower tax rates will start taking effect. That means that Hoosiers across the state will start seeing larger paychecks. And as _**your**_ Indiana Republican Party, we thought it was important that Hoosiers know who's to thank -- and who isn't -- for putting more money in their pockets.

That's why this week we debuted "My Tax Cut," a one-minute radio ad featuring two real Hoosiers -- not actors -- who will both see thousands of dollars in savings thanks to the passage of the Tax Cuts & Jobs Act supported by President Trump and Hoosier Republicans -- and no thanks to Democrat Joe Donnelly, who voted against cutting Hoosiers' taxes.

The ad asks a big question: _Why didn't Democrat Donnelly tell us the truth? Why did he tell Hoosiers that tax reform would be unfair to Hoosier families? What's in it for him?_

[Listen to the ad now](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=8a602a4df6&e=1acd9bb3d6), and consider [chipping in to help keep this ad on the air](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=7f9f7264b8&e=1acd9bb3d6). We've put five figures behind it, with a statewide run launching on Monday. But any help you can provide will deliver this message to an even broader audience.

And if you can't chip in, consider sharing the post now on [Facebook](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=d2a3382dd6&e=1acd9bb3d6) or [Twitter](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=cd9d098015&e=1acd9bb3d6) \-- where it's already received more than 15,000 views -- to make sure Hoosiers know that if Donnelly and his DC boss Chuck Schumer had their way, Hoosiers would continue paying higher taxes with no sign of financial relief.

-Kyle Hupfer

---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=39814d7984&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| **_Healthy Indiana Plan Earns Federal Approval_** Just hours ago, Governor Eric Holcomb joined U.S. Health and Human Services Secretary (and fellow Hoosier!) Alex Azar in Indianapolis to announce that Indiana has received approval for enhancing the Healthy Indiana Plan, which provides health coverage to more than 400,000 Hoosier adults.

This is huge news, as it provides Indiana with up to $80 million annually in funding to provide addiction treatment services to HIP and Medicaid members -- better positioning the state to attack the opioid crisis. And with about 95,000 open job openings across the state today, Hoosiers on HIP will also have expanded access to a program connecting members with job training, education and community engagement activities.

Hoosiers like Governor Holcomb and Secretary Azar are leading the nation when it comes to developing innovative, state-driven solutions to the country's biggest health care problems. If you want to see the latest innovations in health care that can be scaled nationwide, look no further than the leadership from this great team of Hoosiers!

| **_President Trump's State of the Union Address Earns Rave Reviews_**
On Tuesday, President Trump delivered his first State of the Union address, laying out achievements in his first year in office and his priorities and vision looking ahead for America.

In the speech -- which earned approval from a resounding 75% of Americans who tuned in -- President Trump talked about tax cuts for the American people, a $1.5 billion infrastructure plan, his priorities in the immigration debate, fair trade agreements and his focus on defeating ISIS and protecting America.

In case you missed it, you can watch the full speech or read a transcript [here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=cddbf4dab5&e=1acd9bb3d6).

In Case You Missed It:

STATEMENT FROM GOVERNOR PHIL SCOTT ON VERMONT’S DEMOGRAPHIC CRISIS
February 1, 2018

Montpelier, Vt. – Governor Phil Scott issued the following statement regarding attempts by legislators to deny Vermont’s demographic crisis:

"Fellow Vermonters:

"I was asked by a member of the media today at my weekly press conference how I’ve arrived at a calculation I frequently use to highlight our past and present economic and demographic challenges – the phrase I use is ‘6-3-1.’

“For those not aware, this incredible demographic challenge has been pointed out by no fewer than four Governors. The ‘6’ illustrates that from April 2009 to the time I took office, we had, on average, six fewer workers in our workforce every single day. The ‘3’ illustrates that from the start of 1997 to the time I took office, we had, on average, three fewer students enrolled in our K-12 schools every single day. And the ‘1’ illustrates that we saw an average of nearly 1 baby born exposed to addiction every day, in our most recent reporting year (2015).

“Frankly, I was surprised by the question this afternoon, as it has been substantiated numerous times. I have subsequently learned from the press that this inquiry is a direct result of another attempt by legislative leadership — with assistance from their economist — to mislead Vermonters about these facts by intentionally using a smaller timeframe to skew the numbers. I find this deeply disappointing and concerning.

“Cherry picking from a small window to deny the demographic crisis we’re seeing is like claiming that a few subzero days in January indicate climate change isn’t real (which we know is not accurate), because it’s a deliberately imprecise way to try to obscure a clear trend.

“We don't have time for their political games because we have an immediate need to address this crisis. Frankly, it’s this type of partisanship that has allowed this problem to fester and, as a result, we’re just 3-4 years from having only one worker for every child, retiree and dependent of the state, when you exclude the Burlington area. And we’re 7-8 from that bad dependency ratio when you include the Burlington area.

“And, I hear from businesses and non-profits across every corner of the state who don’t have the workers they need to grow, thrive or just keep their doors open. The only conclusion I can draw as to why legislative leaders would ignore these concerns and deny our extended labor force decline is to justify policies they know will not correct the course.

“The size of our workforce as a percent of population is the crux of every problem we face and addressing it will be the cornerstone of every solution. Expanding the workforce is how we generate revenue to invest in our social safety net, clean water, early education, broadband infrastructure and virtually every other priority of our state.

“This isn't Washington, DC. It’s Vermont and I believe Vermonters want their legislators — regardless of party — to get serious about working with me to grow the economy and make Vermont more affordable.”

The data under discussion is included below:

name: John Kasich
subject: A disservice to America
body:
These are some of our country's most honorable and knowledgable
public servants and national security experts. When they speak,
we would be wise to listen.

Peter, in case you missed my
statement today on the release of the Trump/Nunes memo, it's
below:

"The FBI and Department of
Justice are important to our country. While we must maintain
oversight of these institutions, it has to be done in a
bipartisan manner - not for partisan reasons or political gain.
The manner in which this was done was wrong and does a disservice
to our country.

It is very serious when a foreign
power engages in sophisticated efforts to impact not just our
elections, but elections throughout the world. Make no mistake,
Russia is not an ally -- they are not our friends -- and they
shouldn't be treated as such. The independent investigation into
Russia's meddling in our election must continue unimpeded by
outside forces from either Congress or the President."

If you agree, please take a
moment to lend your voice to this cause:

Here are some very respectable
and patriotic Republican voices carrying the same message:

Republican Sen. John McCain,
Former Presidential candidate and war hero:

"The latest attacks on the FBI
and Department of Justice serve no American interests - no
party's, no president's, only Putin's. The American people
deserve to know all of the facts surrounding Russia's ongoing
efforts to subvert our democracy, which is why Special Counsel
Mueller's investigation must proceed unimpeded. Our nation's
elected officials, including the president, must stop looking at
this investigation through the warped lens of politics and
manufacturing partisan sideshows. If we continue to undermine our
own rule of law, we are doing Putin's job for him." (source)

Gen. Michael Hayden, Former CIA,
NSA Director and Principle Deputy Director of National
Intelligence during the Bush Administration:

"The "Release the memo" rallying
cry on social media for a memo produced by Rep. Devin Nunes is
the leading message now being pushed by Russian intelligence
forces, former CIA chief Michael Hayden told a New Albany
audience Thursday.

U.S. intelligence services say
that one of the ways that Russian intelligence meddled in the
election was by using "bot farms" to amplify social media claims
that were helpful to Trump and harmful to his opponent, Democrat
Hillary Clinton. Through that amplification, those stories would
be listed as "trending" on such platforms as Twitter and YouTube,
Hayden said.

"The most trending hashtag on
Kremlin-controlled botnets as we speak is 'Release the memo,'"
Hayden said, eliciting some gasps from the audience of the
Jefferson Series lecture, which was sponsored the New Albany
Community Foundation." (source)

Alberto Gonzales, Former George
W. Bush Attorney General:

"I have no confidence whatsoever
in what's going to come out of the House. Nunes seems to be part
of the Trump team." (source)

--

Peter, these are some of our
country's most honorable and knowledgable public servants and
national security experts. When they speak, we would be wise to
listen.

Please help us spread this
message. Don't do it for me. Do it for America.

Thank you

-John

name: The White House
subject: Resolute Reads
body:
1600 Daily REAL NEWS PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=fbddb86baa2e810fdd81ceb897cd74818fb5672c09acf612c2fe3062132f327f446b26db1f0f061857b664234da3e7577ef961a2fe45c237
Trump tells workers their tax-cut bonuses are ‘not crumbs’

- The Washington Times

On Wednesday, in a meeting with American workers who have benefitted from the recent overhaul of the American tax code led by President Donald J. Trump, the President told workers their bonuses are “not crumbs.” One guest from Iowa even revealed that his family will “pocket an extra $6,000 this year from the tax cuts.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=fbddb86baa2e810f57518cd67627d88f3f7ea6af378d21b26e3cb9305122289425cf61969beccf4c193204a0580c59d5fba671582ffe803d

US oil production tops 10 million barrels a day for first time since 1970

- CNBC

For the first time since 1970, U.S. crude oil production broke 10 million barrels a day. America’s “status is growing” as the world’s third largest oil producer, with production expected to expand. “The Trump administration has embraced energy as an economic force and has declared that the U.S. will be dominant, unlocking new sources of oil domestically,” CNBC reports.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=fbddb86baa2e810fd93959020231b54ef7cb1048765b0f4d8a3d3615564df4c9c2a6d799889f3a2297796328f16ec8ba305ae7cfb1cf884c
Unemployment may fall to 3.5% in a year

- Axios

Moody’s Analytics Chief Economist Mark Zandi predicts that the U.S. jobless rate may reach 3.5% by this time next year, “a level reached in only two other periods since the government began keeping such records in 1948,” Axios reports. Zandi said the current job market is “rip-roaring.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d51d33b9e3398590b8cf96f41dc8d10df81ab1ff498a3726f3b7b544e358ed58f54612ec471c799d2e6d6544819cb70772c577931e6677cc

Cigna raises wages, benefits following tax law

- The Washington Examiner

On Thursday, health insurer Cigna announced it will increase the minimum wage for its employees and raise contributions to retirement accounts “thanks to the passage of the Republican tax law signed by President Trump late last year.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d51d33b9e33985909db033f9e6e36cbefa1262bab4af978b9fbad5db84d7aab799311d95e75115b1d452cee2492bf4e3abdbb90f98e63e99
Popularity of Trump tax reform skyrockets as more Americans realize it cut their taxes

- Breitbart

In a new poll from Monmouth University, the popularity of the new tax reform package is skyrocketing as more Americans realize they will keep more of their hard-earned money. The numbers suggest that tax reform could become even more popular in the coming months.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d51d33b9e33985902e63313054f5864cc059c2b9474dae5be15df3c13e7afd196dd5cfe1c57d4740cd1e4e570e527adb87451bb9770ea932
President Trump invites local news reporters to Oval Office

- Gray DC

This week, the President invited local reporters from across the country to the Oval Office for an off-the-record reception. This event gave local reporters an opportunity to “get down to the local issues that matter to our viewers all across the country,” Gray DC writes.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d51d33b9e33985902f0ca5a9c838a8947e1ec77ba6480396fb31015b5cf5db00145c48b7bf783acdf4d1398d2efa79afff3de22386216ce3
How the U.S. is using terrorists’ smartphones and laptops to defeat them

- USA Today

A profile on the National Media Exploitation Center in USA Today details how the U.S. government is capturing ISIS devices on the battlefields of Iraq and Syria, “providing a huge boost to the U.S. intelligence community as it hunts terrorists.” The data collected here “include videos and photos that help identify militants and their leaders . . . even when a device is damaged or information is deleted.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d51d33b9e339859061322263af75e16139691f8034cc048576d63890658e01203a97816270f5132d60796f60eb4d16a4da17106c726eef8f
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d51d33b9e339859096c382daa4931867579b788ed03f42566f7ad6933fe351de43203a68fa545f3fe15abab16e4f94b6a34addd338ad93a2
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d46d67e6ee2110bdb6bfda9003a2081f6805c045169a2c44bb8e632b93b668a6ec3420670a1db7b0cc090e4f6e3492c6b6b08dde975cd27ce9
Unsubscribe

**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER**
**FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 2nd**
|
---
**_A Note From Jeanie_**
**Dear Derek,**

**Greetings from Washington, D.C.! I’m here attending the Winter Meeting of the Republican National Committee and the tone, tenor, and mood is amazing. Last night RNC members had the great pleasure of hearing directly from President Trump. We learned that together with RNC Chairwoman Ronna McDaniel, the RNC is breaking records when it comes to fundraising -- $132 million – putting the Democrats to shame.**
****
**We have come so far in a very short time with President Trump leading the effort to Make America Great Again. Republicans delivered the biggest set of tax cuts in a generation. Three million Americans have received bonuses and are seeing more in their take-home pay. Unemployment is at a 17 year low, 2.4 million new jobs have been created, job-killing regulations have been cut, our military is stronger, and our veterans are being taken care of.**
****
**Unfortunately, the Democrats hate the President more than they love our country and they continue to obstruct, resist, and divide. On Tuesday night we saw first-hand that hatred. They refused to stand when the President walked into the room. They refused to stand when he talked about the American flag, the National Anthem, or the fact Hispanics and African Americans are enjoying the lowest unemployment rate in years. They even refused to stand for a 12-year boy who put flags on the graves of our veterans. That’s more than disappointing, it’s un-American.**
****
**We have a President who loves this country and is working hard to help everyone achieve their own dream. As the President said in his State of the Union address on Tuesday night, “This is our new American moment. There has never been a better time to start living the American Dream...if you work hard, if you believe in yourself, if you believe in America, then you can dream anything, you can be anything, and together we can achieve anything.” Well said, Mr. President.**
****
**I return to New Hampshire more energized than ever, knowing that together we will return Governor Sununu to the corner office, grow our majorities in the House, Senate and Executive Council and send two Republicans to Washington to help the President make sure that the progress will continue.**

**Sincerely, **

**Jeanie**

clean_body: | | nh.gop NH REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 2nd | A Note From Jeanie Dear Derek, Greetings from Washington, D.C.! I’m here attending the Winter Meeting of the Republican National Committee and the tone, tenor, and mood is amazing. Last night RNC members had the great pleasure of hearing directly from President Trump. We learned that together with RNC Chairwoman Ronna McDaniel, the RNC is breaking records when it comes to fundraising -- $132 million – putting the Democrats to shame. ** We have come so far in a very short time with President Trump leading the effort to Make America Great Again. Republicans delivered the biggest set of tax cuts in a generation. Three million Americans have received bonuses and are seeing more in their take-home pay. Unemployment is at a 17 year low, 2.4 million new jobs have been created, job-killing regulations have been cut, our military is stronger, and our veterans are being taken care of. Unfortunately, the Democrats hate the President more than they love our country and they continue to obstruct, resist, and divide. On Tuesday night we saw first-hand that hatred. They refused to stand when the President walked into the room. They refused to stand when he talked about the American flag, the National Anthem, or the fact Hispanics and African Americans are enjoying the lowest unemployment rate in years. They even refused to stand for a 12-year boy who put flags on the graves of our veterans. That’s more than disappointing, it’s un-American. We have a President who loves this country and is working hard to help everyone achieve their own dream. As the President said in his State of the Union address on Tuesday night, “This is our new American moment. There has never been a better time to start living the American Dream...if you work hard, if you believe in yourself, if you believe in America, then you can dream anything, you can be anything, and together we can achieve anything.” Well said, Mr. President. I return to New Hampshire more energized than ever, knowing that together we will return Governor Sununu to the corner office, grow our majorities in the House, Senate and Executive Council and send two Republicans to Washington to help the President make sure that the progress will continue. Team,

On Tuesday night, Donald Trump spoke about supporting working families.

But his words do not match his actions.

In the past year, President Trump has repeatedly tried to repeal Obamacare and rip away health care coverage from millions of Americans. He passed a shameful tax scam that gives massive tax breaks to the wealthy while raising taxes on millions of middle class families. And now, he's even standing by as his fellow Republicans threaten to cut Medicare and Social Security.

Senate Democrats are fighting back against each and every one of his attacks on working families -- and as chair of the DSCC, I'm grateful to have you in our corner fighting alongside us.

And I know that the best way to stop the disastrous Trump-GOP agenda for good is to flip the Senate majority in 2018.

It won't be easy -- we have 26 Senate seats to defend, including 10 in states Trump won. But we also have amazing challengers like Jacky Rosen in Nevada and Kyrsten Sinema in Arizona -- and we have an incredible community of grassroots supporters like you.

Thank you for all your support before the FEC deadline and for everything you'll do in the months ahead to elect Senate Democrats.

Senator Chris Van Hollen

Hello,

I just got out of my meeting with Interior Secretary Ryan Zinke in Raleigh.

As interior secretary, he is spearheading the Trump administration's offshore oil and gas drilling proposal.

With mayors and other representatives from coastal communities around the table, I asked the secretary face to face to exempt North Carolina from the Trump administration's offshore drilling proposal.

I also let him know that we would sue the federal government if that didn't happen.

[**The Trump administration needs to know that North Carolina's coast is off limits for their drilling proposal. Please, add your name to stand with me.**](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1203686/2fde7c66/474450d7/1569e966/4179800406/VEsE/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/stand-with-roy-not-off-nc-coast?utm_campaign=em18_180203_offshr_adv_gq_ful__rc&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&source=em18_180203_offshr_adv_gq_ful__rc&utm_content=2+-+The+Trump+administration+needs+to+know+t")

Making your voice heard here is important. If we show that North Carolinians are united against drilling off our coast, it could make a real difference.

Our coast is just too important to our state to take these unnecessary risks.

body:
Your input is needed on the design of our official 2018 Women's Senate Network membership card. Will you take a minute to vote?
---
[](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d571/2b07114c/8bd486a1/731cb3d6/1789864995/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/wsn-member-vote?source=PT-20180203-MembershipTeam-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")
Team -- the midterm elections are our chance to take back the Senate -- and support for Democratic women is the key to whether or not we succeed.

And we have the perfect way to show your commitment to re-electing 11 Democratic women Senators -- and adding two more reinforcements: **Become an official 2018 member of the Women 's Senate Network.**

[**But first, help us pick the official WSN membership card that shows your commitment to electing more women. Will you pick your favorite design and pre-order the winner to become an official 2018 member today?**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d571/2b07114c/8bd486a1/731cb3d6/1789864995/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/wsn-member-vote?source=PT-20180203-MembershipTeam-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

[](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d571/2b07114c/8bd486a1/731cb3d6/1789864995/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/wsn-member-vote?source=PT-20180203-MembershipTeam-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

Half of the women in the Senate are up for re-election this year -- and we can't afford to lose a single female Democratic Senator.

So please, become a card-carrying member of the Women's Senate Network and show the world you're committed to helping Democratic women win BIG this November.

[**Team, we know you understand the stakes this year. So please, help us pick the official WSN membership card and become a member today >>**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d571/2b07114c/8bd486a1/731cb3d6/1789864995/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/wsn-member-vote?source=PT-20180203-MembershipTeam-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

Thank you for your support,

DSCC's Women's Senate Network

**A note on donations, team:**

Senate Democrats are committed to standing up for women's rights -- including the ELEVEN Democratic Senate women facing re-election this year. Your gift to the Women's Senate Network helps us re-elect every single one of our Senate women champions to ensure they can keep fighting for us.

[**Contribute**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018a?refcode=DPE-20180203-MembershipTeam-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

**Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee
120 Maryland Ave. NE
Washington, DC 20002** Paid for by the DSCC, dscc.org,
and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
The DSCC is the only committee solely dedicated to supporting Democrats in the U.S. Senate. This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because you are a part of the DSCC's grassroots network. Please add us to your address book or approved senders list to make sure you keep receiving DSCC updates. Emails are a critical way for us to stay in touch with our grassroots supporters. 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. We also strive to keep you updated on issues related to the Democratic Party's values.

If you'd like to take a break from DSCC emails, [click here](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d571/2b07114c/8bd486a1/731cb3d0/1789864995/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/take-an-email-break?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com").

If you'd like to make a contribution to support us, please [click here](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d571/2b07114c/8bd486a1/731cb3d3/1789864995/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/supportdscc?refcode=DPE-disclaimer"). Together, we're going to take back the Senate, but we'll need the help of every single grassroots supporter to do it. If you'd like to stop receiving all emails from the DSCC, please [click here](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d571/2b07114c/8bd486a1/731cb3d2/1789864995/VEsO/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/content/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com"). We greatly appreciate your continued support. Contributions or gifts to the DSCC are not tax deductible.
